_description: SQL Migration Policy Allowlist — historical baseline. ALLOWLIST DOES NOT AUTHORIZE SQL/MIGRATION EXECUTION. Each entry is a pre-existing SQL or migration file classified in python_sql_migration_enforcement_design_phase1. New or modified SQL/migration files with DML/destructive/privilege signals that are NOT in this allowlist will fail CI changed-files enforcement.
_destructive_policy: Any new destructive SQL/migration (DROP DATABASE, DROP TABLE, TRUNCATE, DELETE without WHERE, GRANT ALL, REVOKE) MUST fail changed-files gate even if allowlisted. Manual review required.

entries.reason: M3-D4C additive repair for the persisted canonical observation business fingerprint. No DML, backfill, privilege changes, or destructive operation.
entries.evidence: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN/CONSTRAINT and COMMENT ON only; V26.8 remains unchanged.
entries.review_note: Creation does not authorize execution outside the explicitly authorized D4C ephemeral database.

entries.reason: Schema hardening migration with DDL (ALTER TABLE, CREATE INDEX, CREATE TRIGGER) and data backfill UPDATE. Legitimate historical migration. No destructive operations.
entries.evidence: DDL: ALTER TABLE, CREATE INDEX; DML: UPDATE for data backfill. See SC002_PYTHON_SQL_MIGRATION_ENFORCEMENT_DESIGN.md #1.
entries.review_note: Historical migration. No action needed unless file is modified.

entries.reason: L2 storage hardening with ALTER TABLE only. Pure DDL schema change. No DML.
entries.evidence: DDL: ALTER TABLE only. See SC002_PYTHON_SQL_MIGRATION_ENFORCEMENT_DESIGN.md #2.
entries.review_note: Pure DDL migration. No action needed unless file is modified.

entries.reason: M3-D4B historical odds staging persistence contract. Additive CREATE TABLE/INDEX/COMMENT only; no DML, no backfill, no privilege changes.
entries.evidence: Four odds_historical_* tables; UNIQUE idempotency_key and source hash constraints; no INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.
entries.review_note: File creation is not execution authorization. D4C must validate only in an explicitly authorized ephemeral database.

entries.reason: Docker development environment initialization. Contains full DDL (CREATE TABLE, CREATE EXTENSION, CREATE INDEX) and seed data INSERT. Should only run in Docker dev context. NOT authorized for production.
entries.evidence: DDL: CREATE TABLE, CREATE EXTENSION, CREATE INDEX; DML: INSERT seed data. See design doc #14.
entries.review_note: Docker dev init only. Must not be executed against production DB. Requires explicit env gate.

entries.reason: Creates read-only PostgreSQL user for MCP connection. Contains CREATE USER, GRANT, ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES. Infrastructure SQL for read-only access.
entries.evidence: DDL/Privilege: CREATE USER, GRANT SELECT, ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES. See design doc #15.
entries.review_note: Read-only user creation for MCP. Infrastructure SQL. Should not grant write privileges.

entries.reason: sc002_alembic_migration_runtime_guard_implementation completed. Runtime guard added in run_migrations_online() before any DB engine/connection/context.run_migrations(). ALEMBIC_CTX env var for CI/dev auto-allow. Production-host hard block. Offline mode NOT guarded.
entries.evidence: Guard call at top of run_migrations_online(). Uses python_db_write_guard.py assert_db_write_allowed(operation=CREATE). See SC002_ALEMBIC_MIGRATION_GUARD_DESIGN.md §Guard Strategy Design.
disallowed_operations: Any migration execution without guard (production-like host hard-blocked)
entries.review_note: Guard implemented. All Python write paths now resolved (18 guarded, 2 safe). SC-002 remains partial mitigation only.
